title: City of Berkeley Adopted Capital Improvement Program FY2023-FY2027

verbatim_unfunded_needs.quote: Beyond the projects included in this five-year CIP, the unfunded capital needs total approximately $1.65 billion. Long term funding strategies will need to be developed in the future to meet these infrastructure needs and options will be explored with the City Council and the community.

capital_funding_context.note: The FY2023 and FY2024 capital infusions were explicitly driven by elevated property transfer tax from FY2022 — acknowledged as one-time. The baseline was $2.1M; the infusion was $11-15M.

static_unfunded_needs_finding.description: The $1.65B figure in this document is the same as in the FY2025-2029 CIP adopted two years later.
static_unfunded_needs_finding.prior_reference: The FY2023-2024 operating budget cited a March 2021 report putting unfunded needs at $1.1B for FY2022-2026. The number grew to $1.65B by FY2023 — a $550M increase in unfunded needs in approximately one year.
static_unfunded_needs_finding.scoring_significance: Unfunded infrastructure needs grew $550M between 2021 and 2022 and then held static at $1.65B through 2024 despite $100M in Measure T1 bonds. Bond capacity is being consumed without reducing the backlog.

scoring_implications: Identical $1.65B figure in both the FY2023-2027 and FY2025-2029 CIPs — zero net progress over two full budget cycles.
The FY2023-2024 capital infusion ($11-15M above baseline) was explicitly PTT-driven and acknowledged as one-time. Declining PTT means the capital funding level cannot be sustained.
This document was adopted at the same meeting as the FY2023-2024 operating budget — council voted on both the operating deficit concerns and the $1.65B infrastructure gap simultaneously.
